rationale: In the citing paper, Deleersnyder et al. is used in the hypothesis-development section to support the claim that firms cut marketing effort during recessions, creating potential underinvestment. That claim is directly linked to the paper's recession moderator hypotheses and tests in the meta-analysis. Even without direct method or data reuse, the citation materially informs the conceptual framing of business-cycle effects on marketing's firm-value impact.
relation_description: The citing meta-analysis draws on Deleersnyder et al. (2009) as prior evidence about recession-driven cuts in marketing spending to motivate its hypotheses that recession conditions alter marketing firm-value elasticities. The cited paper serves as conceptual support for why business-cycle context should matter in the model.
